* The function will take a list of integers as input representing weekly rainfall data.  
* It will calculate the total rainfall by summing all the integers in the list.  
* The total rainfall is then divided by the number of weeks to find the average weekly rainfall.  
* The average weekly rainfall is multiplied by 4.345 to convert it to an average monthly rainfall.  
* The result is rounded to two decimal places to ensure it is a float with two decimal digits.  
* The function will return the calculated average monthly rainfall as a float.  
* The example input is [30, 45, 50, 60, 20, 55, 40, 70, 65, 35, 25, 55], which sums to 555 millimeters.  
* The calculation for the example is (555 / 12) * 4.345 = 217.24.  
* The function will handle any valid list of integers representing weekly rainfall data.
